publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 170-171. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest W. I am very busy with my singing & things; & get on nicely, & I think all is doing well.
I wish I had more to say that is amusing; but I am a complete wild Avis, & associate

with but few other Birds. I hope however to be able to sing gloriously by & bye for you; as my voice improves much; & they say that by the Summer I may sing a little to company, provided I choose the right things, and that I am a person very unlikely to be timid about it, (when
I can do it tolerably well); for that I have become so very impudent a Thrush, it is not easy to make me nervous. And how nice it would be for you, if I could help off the evening in this way.
The Hen desired me to mention to you that she has been much relieved about E's cause, by Dr Lushington's assuring
her that Pemberton takes quite the right view of the case, & is all that could be wished.--

Will you kindly, dear, find for me the 1st Vol (an Octavo in Boards) of Lindley Murray's Grammar, in my Sitting Room I believe, & have it sent with the first hamper. We much want it here, for a particular purpose.
